subject: Sports
title: The IOC Bribery Scandal: Salt Lake City
description: A quick win: understand The IOC Bribery Scandal: Salt Lake City.

hook.text: Salt Lake City bought the 2002 Winter Olympics. IOC members received cash, gifts, and scholarships for their votes.

buildup.text: The IOC chooses host cities through a vote of its members—a system ripe for corruption.

discovery.text: In 1998, evidence emerged that Salt Lake City's bid committee had paid over $1 million in bribes to IOC members.

twist.text: Ten IOC members were expelled or resigned. But the Games still went ahead in Salt Lake City as planned.

climax.text: The scandal forced the IOC to reform its bidding process—but critics say corruption has never fully disappeared.

punchline.text: They bought the Olympics. The Games went ahead anyway. Reform came slowly.

quiz.question: What happened after the Salt Lake City bribery scandal?
options: Ten IOC members were expelled or resigned but the Games still went ahead
The 2002 Games were cancelled and moved to another city
No action was taken against anyone involved
